h1. Deploying to UAT

h2. Purpose

This page captures the current UAT deployment process, configuration, validation steps, and known constraints for the Deceased Estate Integration infrastructure deployment.

The main focus is SPESTG-1054: Deploy Integration infra and Resource to UAT.

h2. Current Deployment Scope

The UAT deployment covers the base environment infrastructure required for the integration layer, including Terraform backend usage, environment-specific configuration, Azure resource provisioning, Function App-related settings, and supporting variable library configuration.

h2. Current Status

As of the latest validation:

* UAT backend/storage pipeline has been completed successfully.
* Base environment pipeline backend key handling has been fixed.
* The pipeline now uses the BackendKey from Azure DevOps Library instead of falling back to the hardcoded backend key in main.tf.
* UAT build and Terraform plan have been validated using the correct UAT backend/state.
* Dev and Test were also checked up to Terraform plan to confirm environment-specific backend key routing.
* The actual UAT apply/deployment is still pending PR approval and execution from the standard merge branch path.

h2. Azure DevOps Library Configuration

Environment-specific values are now maintained in Azure DevOps Library variable groups instead of being hardcoded directly in the environment YAML files.

Required variable group values include:

* BackendKey
* BackendResourceGroup
* BackendStorageAccount
* BackendStorageAccountContainer
* BlobServiceUrl
* DataverseTenantId
* DataverseUrl
* FunctionNoProxy
* PipelineNoProxy
* DE_D365_client_id
* DE_D365_client_secret
* DEDIASFTPPrivateKey
* TerraformVersion

h2. Pipeline Configuration Notes

The base environment pipeline passes environment-specific values through the YAML template chain.

High-level flow:

* base-environment-pipeline.yaml
* stage-terraform-approve-and-apply.yml
* terraform-build.yml
* terraform-apply.yml
* Terraform variables
* Terraform local.env
* main.tf resource/app settings

The backend key is now explicitly passed through the pipeline/template chain and used during Terraform init.

h2. Proxy Configuration Notes

There are two separate proxy-related values:

* PipelineNoProxy: used by the pipeline agent/proxy configuration.
* FunctionNoProxy: used by the deployed Function App NO_PROXY app setting.

A validation step has been added so the pipeline fails early if the proxy values are missing or unresolved.

h2. Function App App Settings

The Function App app settings use the Dataverse runtime keys:

* Dataverse:ClientId
* Dataverse:ClientSecret
* Dataverse:TenantId
* Dataverse:Url

Dataverse:ClientId and Dataverse:ClientSecret are backed by the existing D365 Key Vault secret references.

DataverseTenantId and DataverseUrl are now passed from Azure DevOps Library variables into Terraform.

h2. Known Constraints / Notes

* UAT deployment requires PR approval before execution from the standard merge branch path.
* Pipeline execution may require manual approval depending on the configured Azure DevOps environment gates.
* UAT Azure resource visibility depends on the required SailPoint/entitlement access being granted.
* Proxy/noProxy values must be correct per environment to avoid access issues during Terraform execution or Function App runtime access.
* Any apply-stage issue should be fixed through the user branch, validated up to plan where possible, then merged back through the standard process.

h2. Current Next Steps

* Complete PR review and approval.
* Merge changes into the standard deployment branch.
* Execute UAT deployment pipeline.
* Validate deployment result.
* Update this page with final deployment outcome and any additional constraints found during actual apply.


Started documentation for UAT deployment process in Confluence under “5. Deploying to UAT”.

Initial draft now captures the current deployment scope, backend/state handling, Azure DevOps Library variable usage, proxy configuration notes, Function App app settings, known constraints, and next steps. Will update further after the actual UAT apply/deployment result is available.
